coaching.title: Rest Day
coaching.message: Today is a full rest day — and that's the work. You're in Recovery Week (Week 9 of 22), which means your body is consolidating the fitness from the last training block. Resist the urge to sneak in a run. The adaptation happens during rest, not during the session. Do nothing aerobic today.

coaching.title: Fuel for Recovery
coaching.message: Rest day nutrition shifts slightly — drop total carbs to around 220g (your baseline rest target) and keep protein high at 1.6–1.8g/kg to support muscle repair. Prioritize anti-inflammatory foods today: fatty fish, berries, leafy greens, olive oil. Hydrate consistently; many athletes underdrink on rest days without the sweat cue.

coaching.title: Active Recovery
coaching.message: A short 10–15 min walk, a yoga session, or light mobility work is fine and encouraged — just nothing that elevates heart rate meaningfully. Foam roll calves and quads if they're carrying any fatigue from last week. Target 8+ hours of sleep tonight. If your Whoop or Apple Health shows elevated RHR or low HRV, treat it as confirmation the rest is warranted.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Recovery weeks aren't a break from the plan — they are the plan. Elite ultrarunners treat rest days with the same intentionality as hard sessions. 93 days to the Julian Alps. The fitness you've built is compounding right now. Let it.
